item.title: The Hidden Cost of Trump's USAID Cuts | Exclusive Preview
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:
When Donald Trump abruptly dismantled the US Agency for International Development, aid groups and researchers warned millions of lives would be lost. Bloomberg Investigates travels to Kenya to examine the consequences of the cuts by Trump and his deputy, Elon Musk, and how unfinished projects may be creating new dangers.
Dozens of American initiatives designed to provide clean water and protect communities from flooding have been abandoned, leaving some of the country's most vulnerable people at even greater risk than before.
Watch the full documentary: https://bloom.bg/4ylmAaF
Bloomberg Investigates is an award-winning series that sets out to uncover the truth behind some of the most powerful stories from around the world.
#Trump #USAID #Kenya

item.title: How Fast Is the Universe Really Expanding? | PODCAST: The Joy of Why
item.author: Quanta Magazine
item.content:encoded:
One of the most contested issues in astrophysics is our understanding of the Universe’s expansion. Nobel laureate Adam Riess discusses why there is disagreement and what might resolve it.
“The Joy of Why” is a Quanta Magazine podcast about curiosity and the pursuit of knowledge. The mathematician and author Steven Strogatz and the cosmologist and author Janna Levin take turns interviewing leading researchers about the great scientific and mathematical questions of our time. For more episodes visit Quanta Magazine: https://www.quantamagazine.org/tag/the-joy-of-why/
---------
0:00 Intro 3:19 Adam Riess: student to Nobel laureate 5:50 Why supernovae? 10:16 Measuring cosmic distances 15:36 Type Ia supernovae 20:56 Hunting supernovae 25:06 1998: the universe is accelerating 29:47 Einstein's cosmological constant and dark energy 35:52 The Lambda-CDM model 43:13 The Hubble tension: 73 vs. 67 56:35 New telescopes and the joy of mystery
---------
One of the biggest mysteries in cosmology seems to keep getting bigger. Astronomers have known since the 1930s that the universe is expanding, but in the 1990s, the discovery that this expansion is accelerating rather than slowing down came as a huge shock to the field. Something had to be driving that acceleration, and dark energy was proposed as the cause. What began as a seismic shock in cosmology eventually led to a Nobel prize.
But this story has a sequel, and it comes with another major plot twist. The two main methods for estimating the universe's present-day expansion rate are producing significantly different answers. As a result, how fast the universe is really expanding has become a matter of considerable debate — with no small amount of angst — and the discrepancy has become known as the Hubble tension. Perhaps most surprising of all is that one of the loudest voices raising concern is Adam Riess, the astrophysicist whose Nobel Prize-winning work helped ignite the acceleration debate in the first place.
Riess joined co-host Steven Strogatz on The Joy of Why to explain how we got to this point, what the Hubble tension might be telling us, and what may happen next.
---------
The Joy of Why is a podcast from Quanta Magazine, an editorially independent publication supported by the Simons Foundation. Funding decisions by the Simons Foundation have no influence on the selection of topics, guests, or other editorial decisions in this podcast or in Quanta Magazine. The Joy of Why is produced by PRX Productions. The production team is Caitlin Faulds, Jade Abdul-Malik, Genevieve Sponsler, and Merritt Jacob. The executive producer of PRX Productions is Jocelyn Gonzales. Edwin Ochoa is our project manager. From Quanta Magazine, Simon Frantz and Samir Patel provided editorial guidance with support from Samuel Velasco, Simone Barr, and Michael Kanyongolo. Samir Patel is Quanta’s editor-in-chief. The episode art is by Chanelle Nibbelink and our logo is by Jaki King and Kristina Armitage. Special thanks to Garth Avery at the Cornell Broadcast Studio.
--------- Quanta Magazine is an editorially independent publication supported by the Simons Foundation. We focus on developments in mathematics, theoretical physics, theoretical computer science and the basic life sciences.

item.title: AI, Corporate Responsibility & Democratic Legitimacy: Extended Q&A • Joanna Bryson • GOTO 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This Q&A session was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2025. #GOTOcon #GOTOcph https://gotocph.com
Joanna Bryson - Professor of Ethics and Technology, Responsible Data Science for Human Development @j2bryson
RESOURCES https://bsky.app/profile/j2bryson.bsky.social https://mastodon.social/@j2bryson https://twitter.com/j2bryson https://www.linkedin.com/in/bryson https://joanna-bryson.blogspot.com
Links https://digital-markets-act.ec.europa.eu/index_en
DESCRIPTION Joanna Bryson challenges many popular assumptions about AI and AGI. She argues that current generative AI systems are not autonomous intelligences but powerful tools that compress and reproduce human cultural knowledge at scale. While these systems can appear intelligent, they remain dependent on human-created data, corporate incentives, and carefully engineered guardrails. Bryson cautions against anthropomorphizing AI and emphasizes that many concerns about AI stem from misunderstandings about how these systems actually work.
The discussion ultimately centers on responsibility and governance. Bryson argues that transparency, accountability, and democratic oversight matter far more than debates about machine consciousness. Whether discussing AI companions, recommendation algorithms, open-source models, regulation, or AI-assisted software development, her conclusion remains consistent: technology is not destiny. Engineers, companies, policymakers, and citizens all have agency in shaping how AI is deployed, regulated, and integrated into society. [...]
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 00:20 Is AGI just copy-paste? 07:05 Mental health, chatbots & Twitter 21:48 AI, Law & Transparency 31:00 Big data in different industries 34:34 Regulating tech giants 40:23 Teaching critical thinking to the next generations 44:47 Outro
Read the full abstract here: https://gotocph.com/2025/sessions/3783

item.title: URGENT UPDATE - Iran War Expert: A Mass Casualty Attack Is Coming! | Robert Pape
item.author: The Diary Of A CEO
item.content:encoded:
The War everyone assumed was over may be about to become something far worse than we could have expected. Professor Robert Pape explains exactly why the deadliest phase of this war is still ahead, and how this could trigger a wave of terror across the world.
Robert Pape is a Professor of Political Science at the University of Chicago and director of the Chicago Project on Security and Threats. A renowned political scientist, he has advised every White House since 9/11 on military strategy and is the author of the upcoming book “Our Own Worst Enemies: America and the Age of Violent Populism”.
He explains: ■ Why the US bombing campaign failed to topple Iran’s regime, even after killing its top leaders ■ How Iran seized control of 20% of the world’s oil supply within days of the war starting ■ Why the failed agreement handed Iran a path to becoming the most powerful country in the region ■ Why both China and Russia gain from America staying stuck in the Middle East ■ Why he thinks US ground troops could be next ■ Why he thinks there will be a clash between Congress and the president over who really controls the power to go to war
00:00:00 Intro 00:02:42 Why Robert Pape Isn't Surprised 00:04:24 Why the US Blocked the Strait of Hormuz 00:05:43 The Secret JD Vance Peace Deal (MOU) 00:08:33 Iran as Regional Hegemon, Explained 00:10:00 How Do We Know This Is the Real Document? 00:21:25 Did the War Actually Make Iran Stronger? 00:22:26 The Viral Tweet: How Is Iran Still Hitting US Bases? 00:24:57 Trump Says Iran Is Destroyed — So How? 00:25:59 Where Iran Hides Its Drones ("A Truck in California") 00:29:24 Iran Is Building New Drones the Whole Time 00:32:37 Are We on the Verge of a Global Economic Crisis? 00:36:47 What "Owning the L" Would Actually Look Like 00:38:57 Trump: The US Runs Out of Oil in 4 Weeks 00:40:52 The Midterm Elections Time Bomb 00:52:33 The Vietnam Parallel: LBJ's Trap 00:55:26 Can the US Force the Strait of Hormuz Open? 01:03:27 A 70% Chance of Boots on the Ground in Iran 01:14:59 Why Invading (Not Bombing) Sparks Suicide Attacks 01:24:25 When the Two Crises Collide: A New 1960s? 01:25:21 Will Trump Lose the Midterms? 01:27:00 Is the US Running Out of Weapons? 01:29:00 What Is Trump's Real Objective? 01:34:39 Pape's Prediction for the Next Few Months

item.title: Hjalmar Schacht: The Financial Genius Who Funded Hitler’s War Machine Documentary
item.author: The People Profiles
item.content:encoded:
Discover the incredible, chilling story of Hjalmar Schacht, the economic mastermind and central banker who engineered Nazi Germany’s economic miracle and financed Adolf Hitler’s war machine.
In this complete World War II history documentary, we explore how one financial genius ended Germany’s catastrophic hyperinflation, invented the infamous MEFO bills, and secretly bankrolled the Third Reich. How did Hitler’s banker transform a broken Weimar Republic into a military powerhouse? More importantly, how did Schacht go from Reichsbank President and Nazi Minister to a prisoner in a concentration camp—and ultimately walk free at the Nuremberg Trials?
This deep-dive WWII documentary uncovers the dark economics, secret financial schemes, and geopolitical betrayals behind the rise and fall of the Nazi regime. Explore the untold history of war finance, economic manipulation, and the complex figure who outsmarted both Hitler and the Allies.
If you're fascinated by World War 2 history, Nazi Germany, economic history, and untold WWII stories, don't forget to Like, Subscribe, and hit the Notification Bell for more high-quality history documentaries!

item.title: We Finally Know Where Saturn’s Rings Came From
item.author: Astrum
item.content:encoded:
How did Saturn get its rings? Thanks to Displate for sponsoring this video. Use code ASTRUM at checkout for exclusive discounts on your order at https://displate.com/l/astrum. Get 22% off 1 Displate, 27% off 2 Displates, or 33% off 3 Displates. Not valid on Limited Edition Displates.
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Think Saturn was born with rings? Think again. Cassini data proved the iconic ring system formed only recently in Saturn’s life. And in this video, we’ll explore the brand-new 2026 research that explains exactly how it happened.

item.title: The Milwaukee Cannibal - "My Friend Dahmer" | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
The graphic novel "My Friend Dahmer” recounts the early years of a serial killer in the US. This deeply disturbing book by Derf Backderf, a school friend of Dahmer’s, delves into the depths of human madness.
Jeffrey Dahmer, one of the most feared serial killers in US history, was arrested in 1991. He had killed 17 men, most of them young, Black, and gay. When he learned of the crimes, Derf Backderf suddenly found himself confronted with his own past: at secondary school and sixth form, he had been on friendly terms with Dahmer, who was already considered an outsider at the time. To process his shock and make sense of it all, Backderf decided to write a graphic novel — a project spanning almost 20 years. "My Friend Dahmer” became one of the most significant works in the genre.
Drawing on previously unpublished archive footage and testimonies from Backderf’s friends, the documentary offers a deep insight into the long and sometimes painful process that went into creating this extraordinary book. It shows just how difficult it is to tell the story of a troubled teenager who appears to be like so many of his contemporaries, but who has a dark secret. How to maintain the necessary distance without succumbing to leniency or developing empathy for the future perpetrator? And how to confront one’s own past without harboring feelings of guilt? A difficult journey, that ultimately made Backderf one of the most famous contemporary comic artists in the US.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #serialkiller #jeffreydahmer ______

item.title: How H.H. Holmes Became America’s First Serial Killer
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Long before he was hanged in 1896 for murdering his business partner, Herman Webster Mudgett, better known as Dr. Henry Howard Holmes, had built a reputation for fraud, deception, and violence. He also built an infamous "Murder Castle" in Chicago, a labyrinthine building allegedly designed to lure and kill travelers attending the 1893 World's Fair. Historians have confirmed at least nine victims, but many believe the true death toll may have been far, far higher.
On this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift we separate the historical record from the sensational rumors surrounding H. H. Holmes, exploring the crimes, scams, and enduring mysteries that turned him into one of history's most notorious figures.
Do you believe the legends about H.H. Holmes and his"Murder Castle"? Let us know in the comments!
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#

item.title: When Cable TV Came to a Buddhist Kingdom (full documentary) | FRONTLINE/World
item.author: FRONTLINE PBS | Official
item.content:encoded:
An exploration of the impact of television on a remote Buddhist kingdom in the Himalayas. (Aired 2002)
This journalism is made possible by viewers like you. Donate to FRONTLINE now: https://bit.ly/47DFzCb
And support your local PBS station here: https://www.pbs.org/donate
In 1999, Bhutan, a Buddhist kingdom that had remained isolated in the Himalayan mountains for centuries, became the last country in the world to legalize TV.
In the years that followed, Bhutan opened itself to what some critics called “an electronic invasion” — cable television. In this FRONTLINE/World short documentary that aired in 2002, reporters Alexis Bloom and Tshewang Dendup explored the differing reactions to the advent of cable TV in the Buddhist kingdom, including the perspective of a co-owner of a cable service company, who said, “We always have to move forward.”
“Bhutan: The Last Place” was produced by Alexis Bloom, who was also the videographer. The co-producer and videographer was Tshewang Dendup. The founder and executive producer of FRONTLINE was David Fanning.
We are making episodes from FRONTLINE/World’s archive newly available here on YouTube and on pbs.org as part of an effort to make our past reporting widely accessible.
Originally developed by FRONTLINE producers in conjunction with public television stations KQED San Francisco and WGBH Boston, FRONTLINE/World launched in 2002 as a national public TV series that turned its lens on the global community, covering countries and cultures rarely seen on American television. The series completed its final broadcast season in 2010.
Explore more about FRONTLINE/World and this film on the legacy website for the series: https://www.pbs.org/frontlineworld/
#Documentary #Bhutan #Television #Cable

item.title: The Berlin rental rip-off: The housing crisis boom on tiny rooms | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Just under 6 square meters for 600 euros a month. People moving to Berlin often find only overpriced, furnished short-term rooms available. It's all due to the city's housing shortage: Germany has rent cap laws, but they don't apply to temporary, furnished accommodation.
Some landlords even take it a step further. With drywall partitions, they're dividing existing apartments into multiple tiny rooms. What was once a four-bedroom apartment now has eight people crammed into it. It's often younger people moving to Berlin who face the worst of it. Now, the boroughs of Neukölln and Pankow are tightening regulations on room rentals and expanding neighborhood protection measures to crack down on the business model. Along with tenant activists, newcomers are pushing back against high rents and sketchy rental practices. The fight is on for affordable housing and the chance to build a future in Berlin.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #berlin #housingcrisis ______

item.title: Where Did Paying Cards Come From? | World History | Extra History
item.author: Extra History
item.content:encoded:
📚 When you're done learning about the History of Playing Cards, check our "History Happens Every Day" Calendar here - https://bit.ly/2027ExtraHistoryCalendar
The humble deck of playing cards has a history as fascinating as the games themselves. Originating centuries ago in Asia before spreading through the Middle East and into Europe, playing cards evolved into the familiar suits and face cards we know today. Along the way, they reflected changing cultures, politics, and artistic traditions—proving that even a simple deck can tell an extraordinary story.

item.title: The truth about gut health
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Worried about the spread of the diarrhea-causing illness cyclosporiasis? A healthier gut might better prepare your body to deal with it. With all the noise of wellness experts and coaches, we decided to go directly to doctors and researchers who have been studying gut health and the human microbiome for decades.
Gut health is everywhere right now. The hashtag #GutTok has over 6 billion views on TikTok, the global digestive health product market is worth over $60 billion, and viral trends that promise to “hack your gut” through miracle products, cleanses, and restrictive diets are all the rage. But what does gut health really mean, and what can science tell us about how to make sure our gut is in good shape?
With so many voices chiming in on gut health, pushing everything from probiotic foods and supplements to wellness shots and coffee enemas, it can be hard to tell what information is based on real research and what might just be a marketing gimmick or social media clickbait. Vox producer Benjamin Stephen spoke to doctors and visited Cedars-Sinai in Los Angeles to bust some gut health myths for science-backed advice.
Read more about gut health and gut microbiome research:
Vox editor Katherine Courage’s book, Cultured: How Ancient Foods Can Feed Our Microbiome: https://katherinecourage.com/book/
Cedars-Sinai’s Human Microbiome Research Institute: https://www.cedars-sinai.edu/health-sciences-university/research/departments-institutes/medicine/human-microbiome.html
The MAST program at Cedars-Sinai: https://csmast.com/
Trisha Pasricha’s book, You’ve Been Pooping All Wrong: https://www.trishapasricha.com/youvebeenpoopingallwrong
Vox’s explainer video on probiotics in yogurt: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sYj09lx15ik
Pew Research’s findings on why people look to influencers for health and wellness information: https://www.pewresearch.org/data-labs/2026/05/07/why-do-americans-get-information-from-health-and-wellness-influencers-and-how-do-they-find-them/
The Myths and Scientific Realities About Gut Microbiota: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12525918/

item.title: WebAssembly on Kubernetes • Nicolas Frankel • YOW! 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at YOW! Australia 2025. #GOTOcon #YOW https://yowcon.com
Nicolas Fränkel - Developer & Architect @NicolasFrankel
RESOURCES https://bsky.app/profile/frankel.ch https://mastodon.top/@frankel https://twitter.com/nicolas_frankel https://github.com/nfrankel https://www.linkedin.com/in/nicolasfrankel https://blog.frankel.ch
Links https://kubernetes.o https://webassembly.org https://youtu.be/yabE04SD5bQ https://wasmer.io/posts/python-on-the-edge-powered-by-webassembly https://www.fermyon.com/blog/how-to-think-about-wasm https://wasmedge.org/docs/develop/deploy/intro
ABSTRACT WebAssembly started as a technology tailored to web browsers and is becoming popular as a server-side technology as well. The next step is for Wasm to become a powerful tool for cloud-native applications. When combined with Kubernetes, WebAssembly can revolutionize application deployment, security, and resource efficiency in ways traditional containers cannot.
This talk explores why and how to leverage WebAssembly within Kubernetes environments to create scalable, high-performance, and secure applications. [...]

item.title: Who Is Andy Burnham? Why a Former Mayor Is Now Leading Britain | WSJ
item.author: The Wall Street Journal
item.content:encoded:
New U.K. Prime Minister Andy Burnham rose through the ranks of the Labour party to become Prime Minister without winning a single General Election. So how did he do it?

item.title: The global baby bust | The Gray Area
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Birth rates are dropping and global population growth is slowing. Why? We’re heard all the different speculation: It’s the economy. It’s housing costs. It’s the feminists. But what if the biggest driver is that thing we all carry in our pockets?
Subscribe to our channel! http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
In today’s episode, guest host Christine Emba speaks with data journalist John Burn-Murdoch about declining fertility rates across vastly different cultures and economies. They discuss the role smartphones might play in this phenomenon, why governments have struggled to reverse the trend, and how dropping birthrates could change our economy and culture.
Guest Host Christine Emba, columnist and author Guest: John Burn-Murdoch, Chief Data Reporter for The Financial Times

item.title: Alex Hormozi’s Warning: Stop Chasing AI, Build This Instead!
item.author: The Diary Of A CEO
item.content:encoded:
Every founder is being told to build an AI company. Alex Hormozi says almost all of them will be gone within months, that the best move is the exact opposite of what the market is saying, and that in a world of AI the only moat left will be reality and your reputation.
Alex Hormozi is a entrepreneur, investor and author. He is the co-founder of Acquisition.com, a portfolio of 16+ companies generating over $250 million a year, and the author of the $100M series: '$100M Offers', '$100M Leads' and '$100M Money Models', whose 2025 launch sold 2.9 million copies in a single day and broke the Guinness World Record for the fastest-selling non-fiction book in history.
He explains: ◼ Why the practical businesses, like plumbing, are the real opportunity, not the ones built on top of AI models ◼ Why delegating your hardest thinking to a machine makes you weak ◼ Why reality and reputation are the only moats AI can't erode ◼ Why revenue retention matters more than marketing or sales ◼ Why marrying his wife Leila was the best financial decision he ever made, and how the right partner changes your odds ◼ He opens up about one of the hardest years of his life, in the same year he made more money than ever
The views expressed are those of the guest, and this conversation is intended for general informational purposes only. This podcast and its associated materials should not be used as a substitute for professional financial, legal or business advice.

item.title: Couchsurfing in Argentina: Soccer fever, tango and wild Patagonia | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Argentina is in turmoil, as President Milei reshapes the country with radical austerity measures. Subsidies are being cut, the state is shrinking, and reforms are dividing society. What does this mean for the people living there?
The German journalist and travel writer Stephan Orth visited Argentina and travelled through the country couchsurfing. What he found was a divided country going through a tumultuous time: Since Javier Milei took office, Argentina has been experiencing a period of radical transformation. Subsidies are being cut, the public sector drastically downsized, labor rights weakened, and environmental protection laws rolled back — all in the name of a neoliberal shock therapy that has deeply shaken the country.
But despite the crises, Argentinians have not lost their joie de vivre. Buenos Aires remains crazy about soccer, especially during the Superclásico rivalry between Boca Juniors and River Plate. In Rosario, north of Buenos Aires, everyday life is as hectic as ever. The film shows how the economic crisis is affecting people's daily lives in the cities. Meanwhile, in Patagonia, the stunning natural landscape attracts millions of visitors every year. But the country’s transformation can be felt even there.
Between soccer, everyday life in times of crisis, and the sweeping scenery of the Andes, Argentina is a country in transition — divided, contradictory, and full of energy.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #argentina ______

item.title: The Miserable End Of Henry VIII | Last Days
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Ravaged by illness, consumed by paranoia, and fully aware that his reign was coming to an end, King Henry VIII spent his last days doing everything he could to protect his son Edward's future ascent to the throne. But as they often did with Henry... things got bloody and people got executed.
LAST DAYS is Weird History’s prestige format series focusing on final chapters, deaths, downfalls and conclusions. In this premiere episode, we examine the shocking true story behind Henry VIII's last execution, the brutal politics of the Tudor dynasty, and the dark final days of one of history's most notorious monarchs.
Do you think Henry was paranoid? Or was he right to be worried about Edward's reign? Let un know in the comments.
(Much like real history, this video was produced entirely by human beings.)

item.title: What happened to America's Bioweapons Programs after Nixon?
item.author: The Cold War
item.content:encoded:
Our historical documentary series on the history of the Cold War continues with an examination of what happened to the American biological weapons programs after Richard Nixon brought them to a halt. We look at what a shift to defensive programs actually meant, the impacts to public trust of decades of secrecy and lies, and the ethical debate the US found itself waging.

item.title: Elizabeth & Philip - The Royal Family's Greatest Couple Documentary
item.author: The People Profiles
item.content:encoded:
Discover the definitive Queen Elizabeth II and Prince Philip documentary, revealing the untold story of the British monarchy’s most powerful partnership. Behind the glittering gates of Buckingham Palace, Queen Elizabeth and the Duke of Edinburgh navigated 73 years of intense royal duty, global political shifts, historic scandals, and immense personal sacrifices to protect the House of Windsor.
This gripping double biography takes you behind closed doors to uncover the private lives of the world's most famous royal couple. From their forbidden early romance and her sudden coronation to the devastating family crises that threatened the Crown, explore how their extraordinary marriage shaped modern British history.
Through rare archival insights, we reveal the secrets, the quiet struggles, and the enduring love story of the Rebel Prince and the Duty-Bound Queen.
Whether you are a fan of royal history, a British monarchy enthusiast, or searching for the ultimate royal family documentary, this film exposes the real story of Queen Elizabeth and Prince Philip like never before.

item.title: Israel's era of military rule | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
After the founding of the State of Israel, Zvi Elpeleg served as ‘military governor for the control of Arab civilians.’
For decades, Zvi Elpeleg was regarded as a respected public servant and family man. But this image starts to crumble when his granddaughter, filmmaker Danel Elpeleg, starts looking more closely into his past. Based on private letters, official documents, and conversations with people who knew him, a complex story unfolds — one that is both personal and political. The filmmaker not only uncovers a hidden family secret, but also confronts the moral gray zones of Israeli history. How far does the power of the state extend into the lives of its citizens? How does this power shape people's identities across generations? "The Governor” is more than a portrait of a man — it is a critical reflection on control, responsibility, and the mechanisms of state authority. With haunting archival footage, personal interviews, and an intimate narrative style, the result is a multi-layered work that raises questions about power, morality, and memory.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______

item.title: How to Be Right and Lose Everything
item.author: Patrick Boyle
item.content:encoded:
Get an exclusive 15% discount on Saily data plans! Use code BOYLE at checkout. Download Saily app or go to https://saily.com/boyle
South Korea has the best performing stock market in the world for the second year running — and it's also in the middle of one of the worst bear markets on earth. The KOSPI is down around 27% from its June peak, more than 1.2 million retail accounts have been hit with margin calls, and hundreds of thousands of Korean investors have been wiped out entirely. But this isn't a story about meme stocks or worthless companies. Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are enormously profitable businesses at the center of the global AI boom, and the traders buying them were right about the trend. In this video I look at how a national stock index became a two-stock bet on artificial intelligence, how single-stock leveraged ETFs turned ordinary volatility into a mechanical feedback loop, why Korea's retail "ants" took on so much leverage in the first place, and what Victor Haghani's famous biased-coin experiment tells us about how you can be completely right about a market and still lose everything.
